results.title: Fetch | BBB Complaints | Better Business Bureau
results.content:
about REMOVED points from my account. I was given no clear explanation or evidence that justified these actions. This has been incredibly frustrating and disappointing. I earned those points shopping like crazy for the past few days.After looking into this issue, I found many other users reporting the same experience: gift cards being canceled, points disappearing, or accounts being penalized without a clear reason. It is very concerning to see so many people describing similar situations. A rewards program should not encourage people to spend more money only to deny them the rewards they earned when its time to redeem them.I am asking Fetch Rewards to honor my $450 gift card, restore the approximately REMOVED points that were deducted, and provide a clear, detailed explanation for why [...] Fetch does not intentionally limit or disrupt tracking in order to prevent consumers from earning advertised rewards. Reward eligibility is based on qualifying activity being properly attributed and verified through the applicable tracking systems. While we understand the consumers frustration, Fetch is not able to provide consumer-specific internal tracking data, third-party attribution data, or broader platform performance data through the BBB complaint process.

We appreciate the consumers feedback regarding clearer communication when tracking is unavailable or incomplete. Fetch is continually working to improve the Fetch Play experience and the clarity of reward eligibility requirements. [...] out the possibility that unauthorized activity occurred without my knowledge. I have the right under applicable state privacy laws here in REMOVED to request the deletion or anonymization of my personal data. This has become more important to me than ever given the recent security issues I experienced. I have a right to know and access the specific categories and pieces of personal information Fetch collects about me. Which they have not provided, it's just been the same generic messages of "violating their terms of conditions." I have no interest in doing further business with the company and no longer using the app. However, I expect my personal information and account to be deleted or anonymized in accordance with applicable privacy laws. It is frustrating that the company continues to

results.title: Fetch is rated "Poor" with 2.4 / 5 on Trustpilot
results.content:
Evaluating 152 reviews, most reviewers were unhappy with their experience overall. Many customers expressed dissatisfaction with the customer service, describing it as unhelpful and unresponsive, often providing generic excuses or no explanation for issues. People frequently reported problems with the app, such as points being deducted without notification, receipts not being read correctly, or bo nus points for purchases not being awarded. Reviewers also found the process of earning points slow and often had to repeatedly correct receipt scans or product barcodes. However, some customers also noted that when they were able to cash out, the gift cards were legitimate, and some found the app easy to use for general receipt scanning. A few other people also felt that the app was effective [...] Jun 17, 2026

results.title: Social Fetch: Scraper API for TikTok, Instagram, YouTube, X ...
results.content:
Report

Luke

Social Fetch

Social Fetch

Maker

@akbar\_b Appreciate the read on the forum thread, and yes, that flip between clean structured data and silent breakage is exactly what pushed us to build this.

To your questions: fetch is synchronous per call, live data every time, no caching, averages around 3.2s. We track average response time but haven't published p95 yet, can follow up with a real number if that matters for your use case.

On errors, we distinguish these on purpose: not\_found means a real lookup ran and the target genuinely doesn't exist (still charged, it's real work). lookup\_failed and 503 temporarily\_unavailable mean something broke on our side, and those aren't charged. So you can tell "no results" from "scraper's having a bad day" without guessing. [...] Hey Product Hunt 👋 Luke here.  
  
I built Social Fetch because I kept running into the same problem.  
  
Every project that needed public social media data started out simple enough. Grab some YouTube data. Add Twitter. Then someone asks for TikTok. Then LinkedIn. Then Reddit.  
  
Before long, I wasn't building the product anymore, I was maintaining scrapers.  
  
Platforms change. Formats are all different. Something is always breaking.  
  
It felt like a solved problem that nobody had actually solved.  
  
So I spent the last couple of years building Social Fetch: one API for live public data across 20+ platforms. [...] Building an API that pulls data across 13 social platforms, and the thing that's eaten the most engineering time isn't the scraping itself, it's how differently every platform breaks. Rate limits that change without notice, auth flows that expire silently, structured data one week and a wall of JS the next.

Curious what's bitten other builders here. Which platform has been the worst to depend on, and what did you end up doing about it?

View all

5.0

Based on 1 review

results.title: ‎Fetch: America’s Rewards App - Ratings & Reviews - App Store
results.content:
recognizing my email receipts even tho it would scan my email. I got sick of it and stoped using this app all together as 90% of my shopping is e-receipts. Came back months later and it was working again and been using it for a few months now happy again and it started acting up again. This time it’s doing it with all the linked accounts. Sometimes it does recognize and credit me for some however it’s super delayed and will autogenerate them later but only some not all of them. I take all the steps to unlink/link the accounts, update the app and clear cookies and it’s continuously glitchy and frustrating. Esp when I purposely purchased something online to get the pts and it won’t find the receipt! I am in fb groups for fetch customers and this is a ongoing issue with many people. Hello!

Sure, I can help you with that. Here are the steps on how to contact Fetch customer service: 
1.   Go to the Fetch website and click on the "Contact Us" link at the bottom of the page.
2.   On the Contact Us page, select the "I have a question about my points" option.
3.   Enter your name, email address, and phone number.
4.   Describe your issue in detail.
5.   Click on the "Submit" button. [...] A Fetch customer service representative will contact you within 24 hours to help you resolve your issue. Here are some additional information that you may need to provide when contacting Fetch customer service: 
   Your Fetch account number
   The date and time of the transaction where you lost your points
   The amount of points that were lost
   Any other relevant information about the issue

 I hope this helps! 

regards,

Dee
